title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement codeofconduct@damurka.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. title: Data Dimensions
text: Metadata DHIS2 refers predefined data dimensions provide context data values. context essential classifying, organizing, analyzing data, ultimately enabling better understanding information. main metadata dimensions DHIS2 include: Organisation Units: Provide geographical context data. Data Elements: Define measured. Periods: Specify temporal dimension data.

title: Data Dimensions
text: Organisation units DHIS2 represent location larger geographical context data. encompass: Locations services provided (e.g., health facilities, schools, community units). Administrative units representing geographical areas (e.g., district, county, sub-county). Kenya, hierarchy organisation units starts country level, subdivided 47 counties, smaller sub-counties wards. lowest level, individual health facilities community units, forming hierarchical tree. DHIS2 allows classification organisation units using criteria administrative hierarchy. can achieved use organisation unit groups organisation unit group sets. Organisation Unit Groups: Allow additional sub-classification organisation units (e.g., ownership facility level) geographical classification. Organisation Unit Group Sets: Serve umbrella classifications containing individual groups (e.g., Facility Type Organisation Unit Group Set contains Faith-based Organisation, Ministry Health, Private Facilities Organisation Unit Groups).
code: # Retrieves organisation unit groups which belong to the organisation  # unit group set with the name 'Facility Ownership' orgs_groups <- get_organisation_unit_groups(     groupSets.name %.eq% 'Facility Ownership' ) orgs_groups #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   name                                    id          #>   <chr>                                   <chr>       #> 1 Revised Faith Based Organisation (2018) eT1vvFVhLHc #> 2 Revised Ministry of Health (2018)       AaAF5EmS1fk #> 3 Revised Private(2018)                   aRxa6o8GqZN # Retrieves all the organisation unit group sets orgs_group_set <- get_organisation_unit_groupsets() orgs_group_set #> # A tibble: 15 × 2 #>    name                                id          #>    <chr>                               <chr>       #>  1 "ART Central Sites"                 tjVYz9cY7I3 #>  2 "ART Facility Type"                 IwvoiaXmT7G #>  3 "ASAL Counties"                     x8y86riED0O #>  4 "Beyond Zero Clinics"               cUiz93K15ZU #>  5 "Facility Ownership"                JlW9OiK1eR4 #>  6 "Facility Type"                     FSoqQFDES0U #>  7 "Health Facilities -Refugee Camp "  f0JZ8v0Zxa3 #>  8 "KEPH Level "                       sytI3XYbxwE #>  9 "Malaria Epidemiological Zones"     IgwwNhbzeK7 #> 10 "NASCOP Region"                     rbvYf4IjcGf #> 11 "Programme Sponsor"                 OE9Qlwr8XFv #> 12 "Supply Chain"                      Vww46znsPsj #> 13 "TB  Lab Subcounty Store Vers 2022" gmsEbbTJReq #> 14 "TB SubCounty Stores"               R0JQ0AqdsZf #> 15 "TB Zone"                           jvksQn3RnbT

title: Data Dimensions
text: Data elements DHIS2 define represented particular data value. act labels variables describing nature data. Examples include counts malaria cases number women screened. Data elements can represent numerical values can aggregated (summed averaged) monthly quarterly values. can also disaggregated (separated components) aggregated (summed) facility district country. Related data elements grouped logical collection referred data element groups. data element groups directly contain data reference members data elements. Therefore data element groups provide way manage analyze related data elements.
code: # Retrieves all the data elements in DHIS2 data_elements <- get_data_elements() data_elements #> # A tibble: 13,768 × 2 #>    name                                                                    id    #>    <chr>                                                                   <chr> #>  1 ""                                                                      ioUh… #>  2 "0000000 INCOME & EXPENDITURE STATEMENT"                                RvNJ… #>  3 "1000000 Income/Revenue"                                                zo5v… #>  4 "10.1 Residential"                                                      tZ3q… #>  5 "10.1 The facility has a sound fnancial plan that is adequately funded" oYdx… #>  6 "10.2 Commercial"                                                       PQNa… #>  7 "10.2 Financial resources are provided and used towards Quality Manage… OnDA… #>  8 "10.3 Availability of updated fee structure, strategically and promine… UXQg… #>  9 "10.3 Institutional"                                                    FGHs… #> 10 "10.4 Facility improvement/ Quality management funds are effciently us… y9ui… #> # ℹ 13,758 more rows # Retrieves all the data element groups data_element_groups <- get_data_element_groups() data_element_groups #> # A tibble: 244 × 2 #>    name                                           id          #>    <chr>                                          <chr>       #>  1 "706D Haemovigilance Tool"                     EvSFB0kFshj #>  2 "751_BFCI FORM "                               Q3ZqDNTgosn #>  3 "Adverse Events Following Immunization (AEFI)" zHoxBNmNfej #>  4 "AFP Smear"                                    jjm7ipMtusz #>  5 "AMR - Acinetobacter Isolates"                 uqIQDfIInK1 #>  6 "AMR - Escherichia coli isolates"              VjgPbuOqvBq #>  7 "AMR - Klebsiella species isolates"            SMINXpqL0s5 #>  8 "AMR - Pseudomonas aeruginosa isolates"        NpOK7Em3QRk #>  9 "AMR - Staphylococcus aureus Isolates"         B3VgKffq78S #> 10 "Antenatal client first visits"                XTHYIz6qsdX #> # ℹ 234 more rows

title: Data Dimensions
text: Periods crucial factor collecting analyzing data time creating periodic reports. DHIS2 supports two types periods: Relative Periods: Defined relation date analysis (e.g., last year, last month, quarter). Fixed Periods: Refer specific periods (e.g., January 2024, 2020 2024). choice periods depends type insight needed, whether ’s specific point time, trend months, annual overview. details see Date Period Format DHIS2 utilizes multi-dimensional data model, allowing analyze data across various aspects like data elements, periods, organization units. flexibility empowers gain deeper insights answer complex questions.

title: Data Dimensions
text: good news , don’t necessarily need know intricate details every dimension item building analytics queries! khisr provides functions handle complexities .

title: Data Dimensions
text: deeper dives specific dimensions objects, refer comprehensive DHIS2 API documentation

title: Date and Period Format
text: date format use DHIS2 API yyyy-MM-dd. example, express March 20, 2014, must use 2014-03-20. DHIS2 supports two types periods: Relative Periods: Defined relation date analysis (e.g., last year, last month, quarter). Fixed Periods: Refer specific periods (e.g., January 2024, 2020 2024).

title: Date and Period Format
text: period format described following table (also available [get_period_types()]):

title: Date and Period Format
text: can also utilize relative periods addition fixed periods (defined ). available relative period values : THIS_WEEK, LAST_WEEK, LAST_4_WEEKS, LAST_12_WEEKS, LAST_52_WEEKS, THIS_MONTH, LAST_MONTH, THIS_BIMONTH, LAST_BIMONTH, THIS_QUARTER, LAST_QUARTER, THIS_SIX_MONTH, LAST_SIX_MONTH, MONTHS_THIS_YEAR, QUARTERS_THIS_YEAR, THIS_YEAR, MONTHS_LAST_YEAR, QUARTERS_LAST_YEAR, LAST_YEAR, LAST_5_YEARS, LAST_10_YEARS, LAST_10_FINANCIAL_YEARS, LAST_12_MONTHS, LAST_3_MONTHS, LAST_6_BIMONTHS, LAST_4_QUARTERS, LAST_2_SIXMONTHS, THIS_FINANCIAL_YEAR, LAST_FINANCIAL_YEAR, LAST_5_FINANCIAL_YEARS

title: Getting Started
text: Obtain KHIS credentials: typically consist username password, can acquire appropriate channels within KHIS organization. Store credentials securely: khisr provides convenient mechanism storing credentials within R environment. learn set manage credentials effectively, please refer comprehensive guide Set Credentials.
code: # Set the credentials using username and password khis_cred(username = 'KHIS username', password = 'KHIS password')  # Set credentials using configuration path khis_cred(config_path = 'path/to/secret.json')

title: Getting Started
text: filter metadata several filter operations can applied returned list metadata. format filter straight-forward follows pattern property:operator:value, property property metadata want filter , operator comparison operator want perform value value check (operators require value).

title: Getting Started
text: Basic usage metadata filter
code: # Retrieve organisation units by county (level 2) county <- get_organisation_units(level %.eq% '2') county #> # A tibble: 47 × 2 #>    name                   id          #>    <chr>                  <chr>       #>  1 Baringo County         vvOK1BxTbet #>  2 Bomet County           HMNARUV2CW4 #>  3 Bungoma County         KGHhQ5GLd4k #>  4 Busia County           Tvf1zgVZ0K4 #>  5 Elgeyo Marakwet County MqnLxQBigG0 #>  6 Embu County            PFu8alU2KWG #>  7 Garissa County         uyOrcHZBpW0 #>  8 Homa Bay County        nK0A12Q7MvS #>  9 Isiolo County          bzOfj0iwfDH #> 10 Kajiado County         Hsk1YV8kHkT #> # ℹ 37 more rows  # Retrieve county by name (Mombasa) county <- get_organisation_units(level %.eq% '2',                                  name %.like% 'mombasa') county #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   name           id          #>   <chr>          <chr>       #> 1 Mombasa County wsBsC6gjHvn  data_element_id <- c('cXe64Yk0QMY', 'XEX93uLsAm2')  # Retrieve data elements by ID using operator in data_elements <- get_data_elements(id %.in% data_element_id) data_elements #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   name         id          #>   <chr>        <chr>       #> 1 CBE-Abnormal XEX93uLsAm2 #> 2 CBE-Normal   cXe64Yk0QMY  # Retrieve data elements by filtering using dataElementGroups data_elements <- get_data_elements(dataElementGroups.name %.like% 'moh 705') data_elements #> # A tibble: 96 × 2 #>    name                         id          #>    <chr>                        <chr>       #>  1 Abortion                     IrWSgk9GsUm #>  2 All other diseases           KxT47tbKHsd #>  3 Anaemia cases                kkUHOwGMawD #>  4 Arthritis, Joint pains etc.  waNhWrS3HL6 #>  5 Asthma                       L82lvvxVaqt #>  6 Autism                       L529r3Wvtcf #>  7 Bilharzia  (Schistosomiasis) ojFSHMwbkHK #>  8 Brucellosis                  nb9cfWgxYFc #>  9 Burns                        dkEYL9Sous9 #> 10 Cardiovascular conditions    sZETzNe1To8 #> # ℹ 86 more rows

title: Getting Started
text: analytics resource DHIS2 empowers access analyze aggregated data across various dimensions. effectively leverage resource, let’s explore key functions parameters involved:

title: Getting Started
text: get_analytics(): Retrieves aggregated data based specified dimensions filters. analytics_dimension(): Constructs dimensions queries, ensuring accurate data retrieval. %.d% (infix operator): Convenient shorthand creating dimension filters. %.f% (infix operator): Convenient shorthand creating filter dimensions.

title: Getting Started
text: dimension query parameter defines dimensions included analytics query. number dimensions can specified. dimension parameter repeated dimension include query response. query response can potentially contain aggregated values combinations specified dimension items. fixed dimensions data element (dx) period (time) (pe) organisation unit (ou) dimension. can dynamically add dimensions categories, data element group sets organisation unit group sets.

title: Getting Started
text: filter parameter defines dimensions used filters data retrieved analytics query. number filters can specified. filter parameter repeated filter use query. filter differs dimension filter dimensions part query response content, aggregated values response collapsed filter dimensions. words, data response aggregated filter dimensions, filters included dimensions actual response.

title: Getting Started
text: Specify Dimensions: Use dimensions parameter define dimensions want include query response. Leverage infix operators %.d% concise readable code. Apply Filters: Use filters parameter specify dimensions filtering data without including response.
code: # To include a list dimensions for data elements id, dataset ids dx %.d% c('dimension-id-1', 'dimension-id-2') #> <spliced> #> $dimension #> [1] "dx:dimension-id-1;dimension-id-2"  pe %.d% 'LAST_YEAR' #> <spliced> #> $dimension #> [1] "pe:LAST_YEAR"  ou %.d% 'USER_ORGUNIT' #> <spliced> #> $dimension #> [1] "ou:USER_ORGUNIT"  # showing in the analytics get_analytics(     dx %.d% c('siOyOiOJpI8', 'Lt0FqtnHraW', 'OoakJhWiyZp'),     pe %.d% 'LAST_YEAR',     ou %.d% c('qKzosKQPl6G') ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   dx          pe    ou          value #>   <chr>       <chr> <chr>       <dbl> #> 1 OoakJhWiyZp 2023  qKzosKQPl6G  5005 #> 2 Lt0FqtnHraW 2023  qKzosKQPl6G 30915 #> 3 siOyOiOJpI8 2023  qKzosKQPl6G 20443  # Using the startDate and endDate with organisation unit keyword 'USER_ORGUNIT' get_analytics(     dx %.d% c('siOyOiOJpI8', 'Lt0FqtnHraW', 'OoakJhWiyZp'),     ou %.d% 'USER_ORGUNIT',     pe %.d% 'all',     startDate = '2023-07-01',     endDate = '2023-12-31' ) #> # A tibble: 18 × 4 #>    dx          ou          pe       value #>    <chr>       <chr>       <chr>    <dbl> #>  1 siOyOiOJpI8 HfVjCurKxh2 202310  749124 #>  2 siOyOiOJpI8 HfVjCurKxh2 202312  638876 #>  3 Lt0FqtnHraW HfVjCurKxh2 202307 1122218 #>  4 OoakJhWiyZp HfVjCurKxh2 202308  367686 #>  5 siOyOiOJpI8 HfVjCurKxh2 202311  663303 #>  6 Lt0FqtnHraW HfVjCurKxh2 202308  957571 #>  7 OoakJhWiyZp HfVjCurKxh2 202307  432306 #>  8 Lt0FqtnHraW HfVjCurKxh2 202312  760939 #>  9 OoakJhWiyZp HfVjCurKxh2 202312  294421 #> 10 Lt0FqtnHraW HfVjCurKxh2 202310  839071 #> 11 Lt0FqtnHraW HfVjCurKxh2 202311  802956 #> 12 OoakJhWiyZp HfVjCurKxh2 202309  329947 #> 13 siOyOiOJpI8 HfVjCurKxh2 202307  995079 #> 14 siOyOiOJpI8 HfVjCurKxh2 202309  842549 #> 15 OoakJhWiyZp HfVjCurKxh2 202311  260530 #> 16 siOyOiOJpI8 HfVjCurKxh2 202308  855316 #> 17 OoakJhWiyZp HfVjCurKxh2 202310  257604 #> 18 Lt0FqtnHraW HfVjCurKxh2 202309  941409 # Filter by period pe %.f% 'LAST_YEAR' #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "pe:LAST_YEAR"  # Filter by organisation unit ou %.f% 'USER_ORGUNIT' #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "ou:USER_ORGUNIT"  # showing in the analytics. filter by organisation unit with id 'qKzosKQPl6G' # and period 'LAST_YEAR' get_analytics(     dx %.d% c('siOyOiOJpI8', 'Lt0FqtnHraW', 'OoakJhWiyZp'),     pe %.f% 'LAST_YEAR',     ou %.f% 'qKzosKQPl6G' ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   dx          value #>   <chr>       <dbl> #> 1 OoakJhWiyZp  5005 #> 2 siOyOiOJpI8 20443 #> 3 Lt0FqtnHraW 30915

title: Set your credentials
text: Kenya Health Information System (KHIS) comprehensive web-based platform used Kenya data collection, management, analysis healthcare sector. However, need credential access data. khisr make call KHIS without credential provided wrong credential receive unauthorized error KHIS. Preferred method: Provide path JSON file created expected format JSON file Confirm success credential set
code: khis_cred(config_path = "/path/to/the/JSON/file.json") {   "credentials": {     "username": "your-khis-username",     "password": "your-khis-password"   } } khis_has_cred()

title: Set your credentials
text: access JSON file can provide username password directly get username logged users clear password logged users
code: khis_cred(username = 'your_username', password = 'your_password') khis_username() khis_cred_clear()

what: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
text: khisr package designed seamlessly integrate Kenya Health Information System (KHIS), providing R users powerful interface efficient data retrieval. KHIS cornerstone health information management Kenya, khisr simplifies process accessing working KHIS data directly within R environment.

title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
text: can install release version khisr CRAN : development version khisr like :
code: install.packages("khisr") #install.packages('pak') pak::pak('damurka/khisr')

title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
code: library("khisr")

title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
text: khisr , default, help interact KHIS authenticated user. calling function makes API call need credentials KHIS. expected set credential download data. See article set credentials setting credential can invoke function download data API. overview, ’ve logged KHIS specific user hidden chunk.
code: # Set the credentials using username and password khis_cred(username = 'KHIS username', password = 'KHIS password')  # Set credentials using configuration path khis_cred(config_path = 'path/to/secret.json')

title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
text: basic example shows solve common problem:
code: # Retrieve the organisation units by county (level 2) counties <- get_organisation_units(level %.eq% '2') counties #> # A tibble: 47 × 2 #>    name                   id          #>    <chr>                  <chr>       #>  1 Baringo County         vvOK1BxTbet #>  2 Bomet County           HMNARUV2CW4 #>  3 Bungoma County         KGHhQ5GLd4k #>  4 Busia County           Tvf1zgVZ0K4 #>  5 Elgeyo Marakwet County MqnLxQBigG0 #>  6 Embu County            PFu8alU2KWG #>  7 Garissa County         uyOrcHZBpW0 #>  8 Homa Bay County        nK0A12Q7MvS #>  9 Isiolo County          bzOfj0iwfDH #> 10 Kajiado County         Hsk1YV8kHkT #> # ℹ 37 more rows  # Retrieve organisation units by name (level included to ensure it refers to county) kiambu_county <- get_organisation_units(level %.eq% '2',                                          name %.like% 'Kiambu') kiambu_county #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   name          id          #>   <chr>         <chr>       #> 1 Kiambu County qKzosKQPl6G  # Retrieve all data elements by data element group for outpatient (data element group name MOH 705) moh_705 <- get_data_elements(dataElementGroups.name %.like% 'moh 705') moh_705 #> # A tibble: 96 × 2 #>    name                         id          #>    <chr>                        <chr>       #>  1 Abortion                     IrWSgk9GsUm #>  2 All other diseases           KxT47tbKHsd #>  3 Anaemia cases                kkUHOwGMawD #>  4 Arthritis, Joint pains etc.  waNhWrS3HL6 #>  5 Asthma                       L82lvvxVaqt #>  6 Autism                       L529r3Wvtcf #>  7 Bilharzia  (Schistosomiasis) ojFSHMwbkHK #>  8 Brucellosis                  nb9cfWgxYFc #>  9 Burns                        dkEYL9Sous9 #> 10 Cardiovascular conditions    sZETzNe1To8 #> # ℹ 86 more rows  # Filter the data element to element that contain malaria malaria <- get_data_elements(dataElementGroups.name %.like% 'moh 705',                               name %.like% 'malaria') malaria #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   name                                    id          #>   <chr>                                   <chr>       #> 1 Confirmed Malaria (only Positive cases) OoakJhWiyZp #> 2 Malaria in pregnancy                    gvZmXInRLuD #> 3 MOH 705A Rev 2020_ Tested for Malaria   siOyOiOJpI8 #> 4 Suspected  Malaria                      Lt0FqtnHraW  # Retrieve data for malaria in Kiambu county in the outpatient data element groups data <- get_analytics(         dx %.d% malaria$id,         pe %.d% 'LAST_YEAR',         ou %.f% kiambu_county$id     ) %>%     left_join(malaria, by = c('dx'='id')) data #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   dx          pe    value name                                    #>   <chr>       <chr> <dbl> <chr>                                   #> 1 Lt0FqtnHraW 2023  30915 Suspected  Malaria                      #> 2 OoakJhWiyZp 2023   5005 Confirmed Malaria (only Positive cases) #> 3 siOyOiOJpI8 2023  20443 MOH 705A Rev 2020_ Tested for Malaria   #> 4 gvZmXInRLuD 2023    433 Malaria in pregnancy

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
text: Constructs dimensions expression analytics queries based specified property, operator, values.

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
code: analytics_dimension(property, operator, values)  operator %.d% values  operator %.f% values

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
text: property character string representing whether dimension filter. accepts 'dimension', 'filter'. operator character string representing property filter (e.g., 'dx', 'pe', 'ou'). values vector values semi-colon separated string items.

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
text: spliced list filter format property=operator:value

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
text: DHIS2 organizes data using multiple dimensions, unique identifier set items represent specific data points within dimension. Data elements (dx): Indicators, data set reporting rate metrics, data element operands, program indicators, program data elements, program attributes, validation rules. Periods (pe): ISO periods (e.g., 202401) relative periods (e.g., LAST_WEEK). Organisation unit hierarchy (ou): Specific health facilities, districts, countries, keywords user location sub-units. Category option combinations (co): Category option combo identifiers. Attribute option combinations (ao): Category option combo identifiers. Categories: Category option identifiers. Data element group sets: Data element group identifiers. Organisation unit group sets: Organisation unit group identifiers. infix operator used filter dimension includes: %.d%: Infix operator constructing dimension filters. Equivalent calling analytics_dimension("dimension", ...). %.f%: Infix operator constructing filter filters. Equivalent calling analytics_dimension("filter", ...).

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
code: # Create a dimension for data element "DE_1234" analytics_dimension('dimension', "dx", "DE_1234") #> <spliced> #> $dimension #> [1] "dx:DE_1234" #>   # Equivalent to the expression above dx %.d% "DE_1234" #> <spliced> #> $dimension #> [1] "dx:DE_1234" #>   # Create a filter dimension for the period of January 2024 pe %.f% "202401" #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "pe:202401" #>   # Create filter dimension for periods "202401" and "202402": analytics_dimension("filter", "pe", c("202401", "202402")) #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "pe:202401;202402" #>

what: Retrieves Analytics Table Data from KHIS — get_analytics
title: Retrieves Analytics Table Data from KHIS — get_analytics
text: get_analytics() fetches data KHIS analytics data tables given period data element(s), without performing aggregation.

title: Retrieves Analytics Table Data from KHIS — get_analytics
code: get_analytics(   ...,   return_type = c("uid", "name"),   retry = 2,   verbosity = 0,   timeout = 60 )

title: Retrieves Analytics Table Data from KHIS — get_analytics
text: ... title: Retrieves Analytics Table Data from KHIS — get_analytics

title: Retrieves Analytics Table Data from KHIS — get_analytics
code: # Clinical Breast Examination data elements # XEX93uLsAm2 = CBE Abnormal # cXe64Yk0QMY = CBE Normal element_id = c('cXe64Yk0QMY', 'XEX93uLsAm2')  # Download data from February 2023 to current date data <- get_analytics(dx %.d% element_id, pe %.d% 'LAST_MONTH') data #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   dx          pe     value #>   <chr>       <chr>  <dbl> #> 1 cXe64Yk0QMY 202401  1023

title: khisr Configuration — khis-configuration
text: aspects khisr behaviour can controlled via option.

title: khisr Configuration — khis-configuration
code: with_khis_quiet(code)  local_khis_quiet(env = parent.frame())

title: khisr Configuration — khis-configuration
text: code Code execute quietly env environment use scoping

title: khisr Configuration — khis-configuration
text: return value, called side effects return value, called side effects return value, called side effects

title: khisr Configuration — khis-configuration
text: khis_quiet option can used suppress messages form khisr. default, khisr always messages, .e. quiet. set khis_quiet TRUE suppress message, one means, order decreasing scope: Put options(khis_quiet = TRUE) start-file, .Rprofile,  R script. Use local_khis_quiet() silence khisr specific scope. Use with_khis_quite run small bit code silently. local_khis_quiet with_khis follow conventions withr package.

title: khisr Configuration — khis-configuration
code: # message: "The credentials have been set." khis_cred(username = 'username', password = 'password') #> ℹ The credentials have been set.  # suppress messages for a small amount of code with_khis_quiet(   khis_cred(username = 'username', password = 'password') ) # message: "The credentials have been set." khis_cred(username = 'username', password = 'password') #> ℹ The credentials have been set.  # suppress messages for a in a specific scope local_khis_quiet()  # no message khis_cred(username = 'username', password = 'password') #> ℹ The credentials have been set.  # clear credentials khis_cred_clear()

title: Sets the KHIS Credentials — khis_cred
text: config_path optional parameters contains path configuration file username password. username KHIS username. Can optional config_path already provided password KHIS password. Can optional config_path already provided. base_url base url used KHIS instance. default NULL https://hiskenya.org/api

text: return value

title: Sets the KHIS Credentials — khis_cred
text: credentials can provided using configuration file (secure) providing username password arguments. config_path considered secure since credentials appear code.

title: Sets the KHIS Credentials — khis_cred

title: Are There Credentials on Hand? — khis_has_cred
code: # Set the credentials khis_cred(username = 'KHIS username', password = 'KHIS password') #> ℹ The credentials have been set.  # Check if credentials available. Expect TRUE khis_has_cred() #> [1] TRUE  # Clear credentials khis_cred_clear()  # Check if credentials available. Expect FALSE khis_has_cred() #> [1] FALSE

title: Produces the Configured Username — khis_username

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
text: Formats metadata filter DHIS 2 comparison operators.

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
code: metadata_filter(property, operator, values, call = caller_env())  property %.eq% values  property %.ieq% values  property %.~eq% values  property %.ne% values  property %.Like% values  property %.~Like% values  property %.^Like% values  property %.~^Like% values  property %.Like$% values  property %.~Like$% values  property %.like% values  property %.~like% values  property %.^like% values  property %.~^like% values  property %.like$% values  property %.~like$% values  property %.gt% values  property %.ge% values  property %.lt% values  property %.le% values  property %.token% values  property %.~token% values  property %.in% values  property %.~in% values

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
text: property property metadata want filter operator comparison operator want perform values value check call description

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
text: spliced list filter format property:operator:value

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
text: filter metadata several filter operations can applied returned list metadata. format filter straight-forward follows pattern property:operator:value, property property metadata want filter , operator comparison operator want perform value value check (operators require value). view operator see DHIS 2 Operator %.eq%        - Equality %.ieq%       - Case insensitive string, match exact %.~eq%       - Inequality %.ne%        - Inequality %.Like%      - Case sensitive string, match anywhere %.~Like%     - Case sensitive string, match anywhere %.^Like%     - Case sensitive string, match start %.~^Like%    - Case sensitive string, match start %.Like$%     - Case sensitive string, match end %.~Like$%    - Case sensitive string, match end %.like%      - Case insensitive string, match anywhere %.~like%     - Case insensitive string, match anywhere %.^like%    - Case insensitive string, match start %.~^like%    - Case insensitive string, match start %.like$%     - Case insensitive string, match end %.~like$%    - Case insensitive string, match end %.gt%        - Greater %.ge%        - Greater equal %.lt%        - Less %.le%        - Less equal %.token%     - Match multiple tokens search property %.~token%    - match multiple tokens search property %.%        - Find objects matching 1 values %.~%       - Find objects matching 1 values

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
code: # Generate an equality filter id %.eq% 'element_id' #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "id:eq:element_id" #>   # Finding multiple ids 'id' %.in% c('id1', 'id2', 'id3') #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "id:in:[id1,id2,id3]" #>   # Get all data elements which have a data set with id ID1 'dataSetElements.dataSet.id' %.eq% 'ID1' #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "dataSetElements.dataSet.id:eq:ID1" #>   # get data elements which are members of the ANC data element group 'dataElementGroups.id' %.eq% 'qfxEYY9xAl6' #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "dataElementGroups.id:eq:qfxEYY9xAl6" #>   # Get data elements which have any option set metadata_filter('optionSet', '!null', NULL) #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "optionSet:!null" #>
